talk_metadata.talk_title: One Gene at a Time: Metabolic Adaptations Driving Metastasis
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "We haven't studied its role in metastasis before."
Speaker said: "Massive upregulation in both the liver and the lung mets"
Speaker said: "We see no effect on 2D if we eliminate [GSTT1], but colonies disappear in 3D"
Speaker said: "We are still looking for the endogenous substrate for GSTT1"
Speaker said: "We still don't know how nuclear formaldehyde is detoxified"

key_data_gaps: No in vivo efficacy data for anti‑GSTT1 antibodies
No pharmacokinetic or toxicity data for candidate antibodies
No human biomarker validation for GSTT1 or SLC30A4 expression in circulating tumor cells

crispro_opportunity.description: GSTT1 is exclusively expressed in metastatic lesions and absent in primary tumors, representing a metastasis‑specific biomarker that could be used to select patients for targeted therapy.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "Massive upregulation in both the liver and the lung mets" and "no expression in the primary tumors"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can develop a predictive model linking GSTT1 expression patterns to metastatic risk using transcriptomic and imaging data.

crispro_opportunity.description: Serine addiction mediated by SLC30A4 transporter in metastatic PDAC suggests targeting serine uptake or downstream metabolism to overcome resistance to standard therapies.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "Metastatic cells cannot grow if you don't have exogenous serine" and "overexpression of SLC30A4 increases serine uptake"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can model serine flux and predict combinatorial interventions (e.g., serine‑depleting diets or transporter antibodies) that sensitize tumors.

crispro_opportunity.description: Antibodies against GSTT1 are in early development but lack preclinical efficacy data; CrisPRO could prioritize antibody formats and dosing regimens to accelerate validation.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "We are moving with three of these top antibodies now, and hopefully to convince some companies to give us millions so we can develop these antibodies"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can simulate antibody‑target engagement and predict optimal affinity/half‑life parameters for anti‑GSTT1 therapeutics.

crispro_opportunity.description: MAT2A nuclear translocation in metastasis reveals a novel subcellular compartmentalization that could be exploited by CrisPRO’s spatial‑omics integration to identify druggable interfaces.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "We saw MAT2A in the cytoplasm in primary tumors, but clearly moving into the nucleus in the metastatic cells"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can integrate subcellular proteomics with metabolic modeling to pinpoint inhibitors that block nuclear MAT2A activity.

cited_competitors.name: Twist Biosciences
cited_competitors.context: collaborating to develop antibodies against SLC30A4

cited_competitors.name: DepMap
cited_competitors.context: used to identify PDAC‑specific dependencies such as MAT2A

unresolved_questions: What transcription factors drive the up‑regulation of GSTT1, SLC30A4, and MAT2A during metastatic colonization?
Can GSTT1‑targeting antibodies eradicate dormant disseminated tumor cells in vivo?
What is the endogenous substrate of GSTT1 that mediates fibronectin glutathionylation?

talk_metadata.institution_or_pharma: University of Oklahoma Health Sciences Center
talk_metadata.talk_title: Targeting Purine Metabolism to Inhibit Metastatic PDAC
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "IMPDH2 is significantly induced in multiple PDAC cohorts and correlates with poor prognosis"
Speaker said: "MPA as a mono‑agent showed no efficacy in a neoadjuvant trial of 12 patients"
Speaker said: "Combination of MPA with sulfasalazine or RSL3 sensitized resistant cells in vitro and reduced liver metastases in mice"
Speaker said: "We observed increased glutathione metabolism and GPX4 dependency in MPA‑resistant cells"

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Lack of targeting of compensatory glutathione metabolism and ferroptosis resistance pathways
vulnerability_identified.unexplained_resistance_quote: Speaker said: "Prolonged exposure to MPA induces an anti‑proliferative response in PDAC cells"

trial_dilution_risk.target_biology: IMPDH2 inhibition to block de novo purine synthesis
trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: Enrolled all‑comer PDAC patients without biomarker selection for IMPDH2 high expression
trial_dilution_risk.missing_biomarker: IMPDH2 expression level or purine‑metabolism signature
trial_dilution_risk.statistical_concern: Small sample size (12 patients) limits power to detect efficacy

trial_dilution_risk.trial_name_or_nct: Preclinical splenic injection metastasis model (no NCT)
trial_dilution_risk.target_biology: Combination of IMPDH inhibition (MPA) with ferroptosis inducers
trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: Used only liver colonization model; did not assess other metastatic sites
trial_dilution_risk.missing_biomarker: Baseline GPX4 or SLC7A11 expression to stratify responders
trial_dilution_risk.statistical_concern: No formal power calculation reported for metastasis burden endpoints

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Existing FDA‑approved drug with known off‑target immunosuppressive effects limits therapeutic window for cancer
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Monotherapy approach without biomarker‑driven patient selection dilutes signal

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed high IMPDH2 expression in metastatic lesions but concluded that MPA monotherapy is ineffective, ignoring the biomarker‑positive subgroup that might benefit

key_data_gaps: No overall survival data for combination therapy
Lack of pharmacodynamic biomarkers in patients
No comparison to standard of care chemotherapy

crispro_opportunity.description: IMPDH2 high‑expressing PDAC patients could be selected for MPA‑based combination therapy.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "IMPDH2 is significantly induced in multiple cohorts and correlates with poor prognosis"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can build a predictive classifier integrating IMPDH2 mRNA/protein levels with clinical outcomes to enrich future trials.

crispro_opportunity.description: Ferroptosis inducers (e.g., sulfasalazine, RSL3) synergize with IMPDH inhibition to overcome resistance.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "Combination of MPA with sulfasalazine or RSL3 sensitized resistant cells and reduced liver metastases"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can simulate metabolic flux under combined IMPDH and GPX4 inhibition to optimize dosing schedules.

crispro_opportunity.description: Design a biomarker‑driven phase 2 trial enrolling only IMPDH2‑high metastatic PDAC patients and stratifying by GPX4/SLC7A11 expression.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker noted lack of biomarker selection as a limitation of prior neoadjuvant trial.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can generate adaptive trial simulations to estimate power given the proposed enrichment criteria.

cited_competitors.name: Mycophenolic acid (MPA)
cited_competitors.context: Existing FDA‑approved IMPDH inhibitor used as monotherapy

cited_competitors.name: Sulfasalazine
cited_competitors.context: FDA‑approved drug repurposed to target SLC7A11 in combination with MPA

cited_competitors.name: RSL3
cited_competitors.context: Tool compound inhibiting GPX4, used to sensitize cells to MPA

unresolved_questions: Will IMPDH2 inhibition be effective in KRAS‑inhibitor‑treated patients?
What is the optimal schedule for combining MPA with ferroptosis inducers?
Do other metastatic sites (lung, peritoneum) show the same purine‑dependency phenotype?

nct_numbers_to_check: Any upcoming trial identifiers for IMPDH2‑targeted combos
companies_to_monitor: Pharma companies with IMPDH inhibitors (e.g., Novartis, Roche)

talk_metadata.institution_or_pharma: University of Texas Southwestern Medical Center
talk_metadata.talk_title: NUDT5‑PPAT Interaction Controls Purine Pathway Choice and Thiopurine Sensitivity
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "NUDT5 physically interacts with PPAT and this interaction is critical for shutting off the de novo pathway"
Speaker said: "The R70A mutation in NUDT5 abolishes PPAT binding and confers resistance to 6‑thioguanine"
Speaker said: "NUDT5 induces PPAT tetramerization, reducing its enzymatic activity"
Speaker said: "Patients with an enhancer variant that lowers NUDT5 expression have lower 6‑MP dose intensity"

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Lack of understanding of the biochemical trigger that promotes NUDT5‑mediated PPAT tetramerization
vulnerability_identified.unexplained_resistance_quote: Speaker said: "We don't know what the biochemical trigger is for NUDT5 to bind PPAT"

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Current data show that catalytic activity is dispensable; targeting protein‑protein interaction may be challenging
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: No small‑molecule inhibitors of the NUDT5‑PPAT interface are available, limiting translational potential

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker demonstrated that NUDT5 catalytic activity is irrelevant for thiopurine sensitivity, yet earlier literature suggested its ADP‑ribose hydrolase function was key

key_data_gaps: No in vivo tumor models testing NUDT5‑PPAT disruption
Absence of structural data for the PPAT‑NUDT5 complex
Lack of patient‑level correlation beyond one ALL cohort

crispro_opportunity.description: CrisPRO can model the PPAT tetramer‑dimer equilibrium and predict small molecules or peptides that stabilize the inactive tetramer via the NUDT5 interface.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "NUDT5 induces PPAT tetramerization" and "R70A mutation abolishes binding and resistance"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Leverage CrisPRO’s protein‑protein interaction simulation to design disruptors of the NUDT5‑PPAT binding pocket.

crispro_opportunity.description: NUDT5 expression levels predict thiopurine toxicity in ALL patients; a predictive model could guide dose intensity.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "Patients with lower NUDT5 expression have lower 6‑MP dose intensity"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Integrate enhancer genotype and NUDT5 expression into a decision‑support tool for personalized thiopurine dosing.

crispro_opportunity.description: Design a trial combining thiopurines with agents that promote PPAT tetramerization (e.g., allosteric modulators) in NUDT5‑high tumors.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker highlighted that PPAT activity is suppressed when NUDT5 binds, enhancing thiopurine efficacy.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Use CrisPRO to simulate metabolic flux under combined PPAT tetramerization and thiopurine treatment to estimate response rates.

cited_competitors.name: Mycophenolic acid (MPA)
cited_competitors.context: Mentioned as an IMPDH inhibitor with limited efficacy, contrasted with thiopurine mechanism

cited_competitors.name: 6‑thioguanine (6TG)
cited_competitors.context: Used as a probe to identify NUDT5‑PPAT pathway

unresolved_questions: What cellular signal (e.g., purine nucleotide levels, PRPP depletion) triggers NUDT5 to bind PPAT?
Does the NUDT5‑PPAT interaction differ across tissue types or cancer subtypes?
Can pharmacologic stabilization of PPAT tetramers be achieved without affecting NUDT5 catalytic activity?

companies_to_monitor: Companies developing PPAT or NUDT5 modulators
